meta.title: Baltimore Catechism No. 2
meta.author: Third Plenary Council of Baltimore
meta.tradition_notes: Issued by the Third Plenary Council of Baltimore (1884, published 1885). No. 2 is the confirmation edition. Questions are numbered to agree with Kinkead's Explanation of the Baltimore Catechism.

provenance.source_edition: Project Gutenberg digitization of the 1885 edition

data.question: Who made the world?
data.answer: God made the world.
data.group: LESSON FIRST: ON THE END OF MAN

data.question: Who is God?
data.answer: God is the Creator of heaven and earth, and of all things.
data.group: LESSON FIRST: ON THE END OF MAN

data.question: What is man?
data.answer: Man is a creature composed of body and soul, and made to the image and likeness of God.
data.group: LESSON FIRST: ON THE END OF MAN

data.question: Is this likeness in the body or in the soul?
data.answer: This likeness is chiefly in the soul.
data.group: LESSON FIRST: ON THE END OF MAN

data.question: How is the soul like to God?
data.answer: The soul is like God because it is a spirit that will never die, and has understanding and free will.
data.group: LESSON FIRST: ON THE END OF MAN

data.question: Why did God make you?
data.answer: God made me to know Him, to love Him, and to serve Him in this world, and to be happy with Him forever in the next.
data.group: LESSON FIRST: ON THE END OF MAN

data.question: Of which must we take more care, our soul or our body?
data.answer: We must take more care of our soul than of our body.
data.group: LESSON FIRST: ON THE END OF MAN

data.question: Why must we take more care of our soul than of our body?
data.answer: We must take more care of our soul than of our body, because in losing our soul we lose God and everlasting happiness.
data.group: LESSON FIRST: ON THE END OF MAN

data.question: What must we do to save our souls?
data.answer: To save our souls we must worship God by faith, hope, and charity; that is, we must believe in Him, hope in Him, and love Him with all our heart.
data.group: LESSON FIRST: ON THE END OF MAN

data.question: How shall we know the things which we are to believe?
data.answer: We shall know the things which we are to believe from the Catholic Church, through which God speaks to us.
data.group: LESSON FIRST: ON THE END OF MAN

data.question: Where shall we find the chief truths which the Church teaches?
data.answer: We shall find the chief truths which the Church teaches in the Apostles' Creed.
data.group: LESSON FIRST: ON THE END OF MAN

data.question: Say the Apostles' Creed.
data.answer: I believe in God, the Father Almighty, Creator of heaven and earth; and in Jesus Christ, His only Son, our Lord; who was conceived by the Holy Ghost, born of the Virgin Mary, suffered under Pontius Pilate, was crucified; died, and was buried. He descended into hell: the third day He arose again from the dead: He ascended into heaven, and sitteth at the right hand of God, the Father Almighty; from thence He shall come to judge the living and the dead. I believe in the Holy Ghost, the Holy Catholic Church, the communion of Saints, the forgiveness of sins, the resurrection of the body, and the life everlasting. Amen.
data.group: LESSON FIRST: ON THE END OF MAN

data.question: What is God?
data.answer: God is a spirit infinitely perfect.
data.group: LESSON SECOND: ON GOD AND HIS PERFECTIONS

data.question: Had God a beginning?
data.answer: God had no beginning; He always was and He always will be.
data.group: LESSON SECOND: ON GOD AND HIS PERFECTIONS

data.question: Where is God?
data.answer: God is everywhere.
data.group: LESSON SECOND: ON GOD AND HIS PERFECTIONS

data.question: If God is everywhere, why do we not see Him?
data.answer: We do not see God, because He is a pure spirit and cannot be seen with bodily eyes.
data.group: LESSON SECOND: ON GOD AND HIS PERFECTIONS

data.question: Does God see us?
data.answer: God sees us and watches over us.
data.group: LESSON SECOND: ON GOD AND HIS PERFECTIONS

data.question: Does God know all things?
data.answer: God knows all things, even our most secret thoughts, words, and actions.
data.group: LESSON SECOND: ON GOD AND HIS PERFECTIONS

data.question: Can God do all things?
data.answer: God can do all things, and nothing is hard or impossible to Him.
data.group: LESSON SECOND: ON GOD AND HIS PERFECTIONS

data.question: Is God just, holy, and merciful?
data.answer: God is all just, all holy, all merciful, as He is infinitely perfect.
data.group: LESSON SECOND: ON GOD AND HIS PERFECTIONS

data.question: Is there but one God?
data.answer: Yes; there is but one God.
data.group: LESSON THIRD: ON THE UNITY AND TRINITY OF GOD

data.question: Why can there be but one God?
data.answer: There can be but one God, because God, being supreme and infinite, cannot have an equal.
data.group: LESSON THIRD: ON THE UNITY AND TRINITY OF GOD

data.question: How many Persons are there in God?
data.answer: In God there are three Divine Persons, really distinct, and equal in all things--the Father, the Son, and the Holy Ghost.
data.group: LESSON THIRD: ON THE UNITY AND TRINITY OF GOD

data.question: Is the Father God?
data.answer: The Father is God and the first Person of the Blessed Trinity.
data.group: LESSON THIRD: ON THE UNITY AND TRINITY OF GOD

data.question: Is the Son God?
data.answer: The Son is God and the second Person of the Blessed Trinity.
data.group: LESSON THIRD: ON THE UNITY AND TRINITY OF GOD

data.question: Is the Holy Ghost God?
data.answer: The Holy Ghost is God and the third Person of the Blessed Trinity.
data.group: LESSON THIRD: ON THE UNITY AND TRINITY OF GOD

data.question: What do you mean by the Blessed Trinity?
data.answer: By the Blessed Trinity I mean one God in three Divine Persons.
data.group: LESSON THIRD: ON THE UNITY AND TRINITY OF GOD

data.question: Are the three Divine Persons equal in all things?
data.answer: The three Divine Persons are equal in all things.
data.group: LESSON THIRD: ON THE UNITY AND TRINITY OF GOD

data.question: Are the three Divine Persons one and the same God?
data.answer: The three Divine Persons are one and the same God, having one and the same Divine nature and substance.
data.group: LESSON THIRD: ON THE UNITY AND TRINITY OF GOD

data.question: Can we fully understand how the three Divine Persons are one and the same God?
data.answer: We cannot fully understand how the three Divine Persons are one and the same God, because this is a mystery.
data.group: LESSON THIRD: ON THE UNITY AND TRINITY OF GOD

data.question: What is a mystery?
data.answer: A mystery is a truth which we cannot fully understand.
data.group: LESSON THIRD: ON THE UNITY AND TRINITY OF GOD

data.question: Who created heaven and earth, and all things?
data.answer: God created heaven and earth, and all things.
data.group: LESSON FOURTH: ON CREATION

data.question: How did God create heaven and earth?
data.answer: God created heaven and earth from nothing by His word only; that is, by a single act of His all-powerful will.
data.group: LESSON FOURTH: ON CREATION

data.question: Which are the chief creatures of God?
data.answer: The chief creatures of God are angels and men.
data.group: LESSON FOURTH: ON CREATION

data.question: What are angels?
data.answer: Angels are pure spirits without a body, created to adore and enjoy God in heaven.
data.group: LESSON FOURTH: ON CREATION

data.question: Were the angels created for any other purpose?
data.answer: The angels were also created to assist before the throne of God and to minister unto Him; they have often been sent as messengers from God to man; and are also appointed our guardians.
data.group: LESSON FOURTH: ON CREATION

data.question: Were the angels, as God created them, good and happy?
data.answer: The angels, as God created them, were good and happy.
data.group: LESSON FOURTH: ON CREATION

data.question: Did all the angels remain good and happy?
data.answer: All the angels did not remain good and happy; many of them sinned and were cast into hell, and these are called devils or bad angels.
data.group: LESSON FOURTH: ON CREATION

data.question: Who were the first man and woman?
data.answer: The first man and woman were Adam and Eve.
data.group: LESSON FIFTH: ON OUR FIRST PARENTS AND THE FALL

data.question: Were Adam and Eve innocent and holy when they came from the hand of God?
data.answer: Adam and Eve were innocent and holy when they came from the hand of God.
data.group: LESSON FIFTH: ON OUR FIRST PARENTS AND THE FALL

data.question: Did God give any command to Adam and Eve?
data.answer: To try their obedience God commanded Adam and Eve not to eat of a certain fruit which grew in the garden of Paradise.
data.group: LESSON FIFTH: ON OUR FIRST PARENTS AND THE FALL

data.question: Which were the chief blessings intended for Adam and Eve had they remained faithful to God?
data.answer: The chief blessings intended for Adam and Eve, had they remained faithful to God, were a constant state of happiness in this life and everlasting glory in the next.
data.group: LESSON FIFTH: ON OUR FIRST PARENTS AND THE FALL

data.question: Did Adam and Eve remain faithful to God?
data.answer: Adam and Eve did not remain faithful to God; but broke His command by eating the forbidden fruit.
data.group: LESSON FIFTH: ON OUR FIRST PARENTS AND THE FALL

data.question: What befell Adam and Eve on account of their sin?
data.answer: Adam and Eve, on account of their sin, lost innocence and holiness, and were doomed to sickness and death.
data.group: LESSON FIFTH: ON OUR FIRST PARENTS AND THE FALL

data.question: What evil befell us on account of the disobedience of our first parents?
data.answer: On account of the disobedience of our first parents, we all share in their sin and punishment, as we should have shared in their happiness if they had remained faithful.
data.group: LESSON FIFTH: ON OUR FIRST PARENTS AND THE FALL

data.question: What other effects followed from the sin of our first parents?
data.answer: Our nature was corrupted by the sin of our first parents, which darkened our understanding, weakened our will, and left in us a strong inclination to evil.
data.group: LESSON FIFTH: ON OUR FIRST PARENTS AND THE FALL

data.question: What is the sin called which we inherit from our first parents?
data.answer: The sin which we inherit from our first parents is called original sin.
data.group: LESSON FIFTH: ON OUR FIRST PARENTS AND THE FALL

data.question: Why is this sin called original?
data.answer: This sin is called original because it comes down to us from our first parents, and we are brought into the world with its guilt on our soul.
data.group: LESSON FIFTH: ON OUR FIRST PARENTS AND THE FALL

data.question: Does this corruption of our nature remain in us after original sin is forgiven?
data.answer: This corruption of our nature and other punishments remain in us after original sin is forgiven.
data.group: LESSON FIFTH: ON OUR FIRST PARENTS AND THE FALL

data.question: Was any one ever preserved from original sin?
data.answer: The Blessed Virgin Mary, through the merits of her Divine Son, was preserved free from the guilt of original sin, and this privilege is called her Immaculate Conception.
data.group: LESSON FIFTH: ON OUR FIRST PARENTS AND THE FALL

data.question: Is original sin the only kind of sin?
data.answer: Original sin is not the only kind of sin; there is another kind of sin, which we commit ourselves, called actual sin.
data.group: LESSON SIXTH: ON SIN AND ITS KINDS

data.question: What is actual sin?
data.answer: Actual sin is any wilful thought, word, deed, or omission contrary to the law of God.
data.group: LESSON SIXTH: ON SIN AND ITS KINDS

data.question: How many kinds of actual sin are there?
data.answer: There are two kinds of actual sin--mortal and venial.
data.group: LESSON SIXTH: ON SIN AND ITS KINDS

data.question: What is mortal sin?
data.answer: Mortal sin is a grievous offense against the law of God.
data.group: LESSON SIXTH: ON SIN AND ITS KINDS

data.question: Why is this sin called mortal?
data.answer: This sin is called mortal because it deprives us of spiritual life, which is sanctifying grace, and brings everlasting death and damnation on the soul.
data.group: LESSON SIXTH: ON SIN AND ITS KINDS

data.question: How many things are necessary to make a sin mortal?
data.answer: To make a sin mortal three things are necessary: a grievous matter, sufficient reflection, and full consent of the will.
data.group: LESSON SIXTH: ON SIN AND ITS KINDS

data.question: What is venial sin?
data.answer: Venial sin is a slight offense against the law of God in matters of less importance, or in matters of great importance it is an offense committed without sufficient reflection or full consent of the will.
data.group: LESSON SIXTH: ON SIN AND ITS KINDS

data.question: Which are the effects of venial sin?
data.answer: The effects of venial sin are the lessening of the love of God in our heart, the making us less worthy of His help, and the weakening of the power to resist mortal sin.
data.group: LESSON SIXTH: ON SIN AND ITS KINDS

data.question: Which are the chief sources of sin?
data.answer: The chief sources of sin are seven: Pride, Covetousness, Lust, Anger, Gluttony, Envy, and Sloth; and they are commonly called capital sins.
data.group: LESSON SIXTH: ON SIN AND ITS KINDS

data.question: Did God abandon man after he fell into sin?
data.answer: God did not abandon man after he fell into sin, but promised him a Redeemer, who was to satisfy for man's sin and reopen to him the gates of heaven.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTH: ON THE INCARNATION AND REDEMPTION

data.question: Who is the Redeemer?
data.answer: Our Blessed Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ is the Redeemer of mankind.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTH: ON THE INCARNATION AND REDEMPTION

data.question: What do you believe of Jesus Christ?
data.answer: I believe that Jesus Christ is the Son of God, the second Person of the Blessed Trinity, true God and true man.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTH: ON THE INCARNATION AND REDEMPTION

data.question: Why is Jesus Christ true God?
data.answer: Jesus Christ is true God because He is the true and only Son of God the Father.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTH: ON THE INCARNATION AND REDEMPTION

data.question: Why is Jesus Christ true man?
data.answer: Jesus Christ is true man because He is the Son of the Blessed Virgin Mary and has a body and soul like ours.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTH: ON THE INCARNATION AND REDEMPTION

data.question: How many natures are there in Jesus Christ?
data.answer: In Jesus Christ there are two natures, the nature of God and the nature of man.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTH: ON THE INCARNATION AND REDEMPTION

data.question: Is Jesus Christ more than one person?
data.answer: No, Jesus Christ is but one Divine Person.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTH: ON THE INCARNATION AND REDEMPTION

data.question: Was Jesus Christ always God?
data.answer: Jesus Christ was always God, as He is the second Person of the Blessed Trinity, equal to His Father from all eternity.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTH: ON THE INCARNATION AND REDEMPTION

data.question: Was Jesus Christ always man?
data.answer: Jesus Christ was not always man, but became man at the time of His Incarnation.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTH: ON THE INCARNATION AND REDEMPTION

data.question: What do you mean by the Incarnation?
data.answer: By the Incarnation I mean that the Son of God was made man.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTH: ON THE INCARNATION AND REDEMPTION

data.question: How was the Son of God made man?
data.answer: The Son of God was conceived and made man by the power of the Holy Ghost, in the womb of the Blessed Virgin Mary.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTH: ON THE INCARNATION AND REDEMPTION

data.question: Is the Blessed Virgin Mary truly the Mother of God?
data.answer: The Blessed Virgin Mary is truly the Mother of God, because the same Divine Person who is the Son of God is also the Son of the Blessed Virgin Mary.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTH: ON THE INCARNATION AND REDEMPTION

data.question: Did the Son of God become man immediately after the sin of our first parents?
data.answer: The Son of God did not become man immediately after the sin of our first parents, but was promised to them as a Redeemer.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTH: ON THE INCARNATION AND REDEMPTION

data.question: How could they be saved who lived before the Son of God became man?
data.answer: They who lived before the Son of God became man could be saved by believing in a Redeemer to come, and by keeping the commandments.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTH: ON THE INCARNATION AND REDEMPTION

data.question: On what day was the Son of God conceived and made man?
data.answer: The Son of God was conceived and made man on Annunciation day--the day on which the Angel Gabriel announced to the Blessed Virgin Mary that she was to be the Mother of God.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTH: ON THE INCARNATION AND REDEMPTION

data.question: On what day was Christ born?
data.answer: Christ was born on Christmas day in a stable at Bethlehem, over nineteen hundred years ago.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTH: ON THE INCARNATION AND REDEMPTION

data.question: How long did Christ live on earth?
data.answer: Christ lived on earth about thirty-three years, and led a most holy life in poverty and suffering.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTH: ON THE INCARNATION AND REDEMPTION

data.question: Why did Christ live so long on earth?
data.answer: Christ lived so long on earth to show us the way to heaven by His teachings and example.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTH: ON THE INCARNATION AND REDEMPTION

data.question: What did Jesus Christ suffer?
data.answer: Jesus Christ suffered a bloody sweat, a cruel scourging, was crowned with thorns, and was crucified.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTH: ON OUR LORD'S PASSION, DEATH, RESURRECTION, AND ASCENSION

data.question: On what day did Christ die?
data.answer: Christ died on Good Friday.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTH: ON OUR LORD'S PASSION, DEATH, RESURRECTION, AND ASCENSION

data.question: Why do you call that day "good" on which Christ died so sorrowful a death?
data.answer: We call that day "good" on which Christ died because by His death He showed His great love for man, and purchased for him every blessing.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTH: ON OUR LORD'S PASSION, DEATH, RESURRECTION, AND ASCENSION

data.question: Where did Christ die?
data.answer: Christ died on Mount Calvary.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTH: ON OUR LORD'S PASSION, DEATH, RESURRECTION, AND ASCENSION

data.question: How did Christ die?
data.answer: Christ was nailed to the Cross and died on it between two thieves.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTH: ON OUR LORD'S PASSION, DEATH, RESURRECTION, AND ASCENSION

data.question: Why did Christ suffer and die?
data.answer: Christ suffered and died for our sins.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTH: ON OUR LORD'S PASSION, DEATH, RESURRECTION, AND ASCENSION

data.question: What lessons do we learn from the sufferings and death of Christ?
data.answer: From the sufferings and death of Christ we learn the great evil of sin, the hatred God bears to it, and the necessity of satisfying for it.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTH: ON OUR LORD'S PASSION, DEATH, RESURRECTION, AND ASCENSION

data.question: Where did Christ's soul go after His death?
data.answer: After Christ's death His soul descended into hell.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTH: ON OUR LORD'S PASSION, DEATH, RESURRECTION, AND ASCENSION

data.question: Did Christ's soul descend into the hell of the damned?
data.answer: The hell into which Christ's soul descended was not the hell of the damned, but a place or state of rest called Limbo, where the souls of the just were waiting for Him.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTH: ON OUR LORD'S PASSION, DEATH, RESURRECTION, AND ASCENSION

data.question: Why did Christ descend into Limbo?
data.answer: Christ descended into Limbo to preach to the souls who were in prison--that is, to announce to them the joyful tidings of their redemption.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTH: ON OUR LORD'S PASSION, DEATH, RESURRECTION, AND ASCENSION

data.question: Where was Christ's body while His soul was in Limbo?
data.answer: While Christ's soul was in Limbo His body was in the holy sepulchre.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTH: ON OUR LORD'S PASSION, DEATH, RESURRECTION, AND ASCENSION

data.question: On what day did Christ rise from the dead?
data.answer: Christ rose from the dead, glorious and immortal, on Easter Sunday, the third day after His death.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTH: ON OUR LORD'S PASSION, DEATH, RESURRECTION, AND ASCENSION

data.question: How long did Christ stay on earth after His resurrection?
data.answer: Christ stayed on earth forty days after His resurrection to show that He was truly risen from the dead, and to instruct His Apostles.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTH: ON OUR LORD'S PASSION, DEATH, RESURRECTION, AND ASCENSION

data.question: After Christ had remained forty days on earth whither did He go?
data.answer: After forty days Christ ascended into heaven, and the day on which He ascended into heaven is called Ascension day.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTH: ON OUR LORD'S PASSION, DEATH, RESURRECTION, AND ASCENSION

data.question: Where is Christ in heaven?
data.answer: In heaven Christ sits at the right hand of God the Father Almighty.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTH: ON OUR LORD'S PASSION, DEATH, RESURRECTION, AND ASCENSION

data.question: What do you mean by saying that Christ sits at the right hand Of God?
data.answer: When I say that Christ sits at the right hand of God I mean that Christ as God is equal to His Father in all things, and that as man He is in the highest place in heaven next to God.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTH: ON OUR LORD'S PASSION, DEATH, RESURRECTION, AND ASCENSION

data.question: Who is the Holy Ghost?
data.answer: The Holy Ghost is the third Person of the Blessed Trinity.
data.group: LESSON NINTH: ON THE HOLY GHOST AND HIS DESCENT UPON THE APOSTLES

data.question: From whom does the Holy Ghost proceed?
data.answer: The Holy Ghost proceeds from the Father and the Son.
data.group: LESSON NINTH: ON THE HOLY GHOST AND HIS DESCENT UPON THE APOSTLES

data.question: Is the Holy Ghost equal to the Father and the Son?
data.answer: The Holy Ghost is equal to the Father and the Son, being the same Lord and God as They are.
data.group: LESSON NINTH: ON THE HOLY GHOST AND HIS DESCENT UPON THE APOSTLES

data.question: On what day did the Holy Ghost come down upon the Apostles?
data.answer: The Holy Ghost came down upon the Apostles ten days after the Ascension of our Lord; and the day on which He came down upon the Apostles is called Whitsunday, or Pentecost.
data.group: LESSON NINTH: ON THE HOLY GHOST AND HIS DESCENT UPON THE APOSTLES

data.question: How did the Holy Ghost come down upon the Apostles?
data.answer: The Holy Ghost came down upon the Apostles in the form of tongues of fire.
data.group: LESSON NINTH: ON THE HOLY GHOST AND HIS DESCENT UPON THE APOSTLES

data.question: Who sent the Holy Ghost upon the Apostles?
data.answer: Our Lord Jesus Christ sent the Holy Ghost upon the Apostles.
data.group: LESSON NINTH: ON THE HOLY GHOST AND HIS DESCENT UPON THE APOSTLES

data.question: Why did Christ send the Holy Ghost?
data.answer: Christ sent the Holy Ghost to sanctify His Church, to enlighten and strengthen the Apostles, and to enable them to preach the Gospel.
data.group: LESSON NINTH: ON THE HOLY GHOST AND HIS DESCENT UPON THE APOSTLES

data.question: Will the Holy Ghost abide with the Church forever?
data.answer: The Holy Ghost will abide with the Church forever, and guide it in the way of holiness and truth.
data.group: LESSON NINTH: ON THE HOLY GHOST AND HIS DESCENT UPON THE APOSTLES

data.question: Which are the chief effects of the Redemption?
data.answer: The chief effects of the Redemption are two: The satisfaction of God's justice by Christ's sufferings and death, and the gaining of grace for men.
data.group: LESSON TENTH: ON THE EFFECTS OF THE REDEMPTION

data.question: What do you mean by grace?
data.answer: By grace I mean a supernatural gift of God bestowed on us, through the merits of Jesus Christ, for our salvation.
data.group: LESSON TENTH: ON THE EFFECTS OF THE REDEMPTION

data.question: How many kinds of grace are there?
data.answer: There are two kinds of grace, sanctifying grace and actual grace.
data.group: LESSON TENTH: ON THE EFFECTS OF THE REDEMPTION

data.question: What is sanctifying grace?
data.answer: Sanctifying grace is that grace which makes the soul holy and pleasing to God.
data.group: LESSON TENTH: ON THE EFFECTS OF THE REDEMPTION

data.question: What do you call those graces or gifts of God by which we believe in Him, hope in Him, and love Him?
data.answer: Those graces or gifts of God by which we believe in Him, and hope in Him, and love Him, are called the Divine virtues of Faith, Hope, and Charity.
data.group: LESSON TENTH: ON THE EFFECTS OF THE REDEMPTION

data.question: What is Faith?
data.answer: Faith is a Divine virtue by which we firmly believe the truths which God has revealed.
data.group: LESSON TENTH: ON THE EFFECTS OF THE REDEMPTION

data.question: What is Hope?
data.answer: Hope is a Divine virtue by which we firmly trust that God will give us eternal life and the means to obtain it.
data.group: LESSON TENTH: ON THE EFFECTS OF THE REDEMPTION

data.question: What is Charity?
data.answer: Charity is a Divine virtue by which we love God above all things for His own sake, and our neighbor as ourselves for the love of God.
data.group: LESSON TENTH: ON THE EFFECTS OF THE REDEMPTION

data.question: What is actual grace?
data.answer: Actual grace is that help of God which enlightens our mind and moves our will to shun evil and do good.
data.group: LESSON TENTH: ON THE EFFECTS OF THE REDEMPTION

data.question: Is grace necessary to salvation?
data.answer: Grace is necessary to salvation, because without grace we can do nothing to merit heaven.
data.group: LESSON TENTH: ON THE EFFECTS OF THE REDEMPTION

data.question: Can we resist the grace of God?
data.answer: We can and unfortunately often do resist the grace of God.
data.group: LESSON TENTH: ON THE EFFECTS OF THE REDEMPTION

data.question: What is the grace of perseverance?
data.answer: The grace of perseverance is a particular gift of God which enables us to continue in the state of grace till death.
data.group: LESSON TENTH: ON THE EFFECTS OF THE REDEMPTION

data.question: Which are the means instituted by our Lord to enable men at all times to share in the fruits of the Redemption?
data.answer: The means instituted by our Lord to enable men at all times to share in the fruits of His Redemption are the Church and the Sacraments.
data.group: LESSON ELEVENTH: ON THE CHURCH

data.question: What is the Church?
data.answer: The Church is the congregation of all those who profess the faith of Christ, partake of the same Sacraments, and are governed by their lawful pastors under one visible head.
data.group: LESSON ELEVENTH: ON THE CHURCH

data.question: Who is the invisible Head of the Church?
data.answer: Jesus Christ is the invisible Head of the Church.
data.group: LESSON ELEVENTH: ON THE CHURCH

data.question: Who is the visible Head of the Church?
data.answer: Our Holy Father the Pope, the Bishop of Rome, is the Vicar of Christ on earth and the visible Head of the Church.
data.group: LESSON ELEVENTH: ON THE CHURCH

data.question: Why is the Pope, the Bishop of Rome, the visible Head of the Church?
data.answer: The Pope, the Bishop of Rome, is the visible Head of the Church because he is the successor of St. Peter, whom Christ made the chief of the Apostles and the visible Head of the Church.
data.group: LESSON ELEVENTH: ON THE CHURCH

data.question: Who are the successors of the other Apostles?
data.answer: The successors of the other Apostles are the bishops of the Holy Catholic Church.
data.group: LESSON ELEVENTH: ON THE CHURCH

data.question: Why did Christ found the Church?
data.answer: Christ founded the Church to teach, govern, sanctify, and save all men.
data.group: LESSON ELEVENTH: ON THE CHURCH

data.question: Are all bound to belong to the Church?
data.answer: All are bound to belong to the Church, and he who knows the Church to be the true Church and remains out of it cannot be saved.
data.group: LESSON ELEVENTH: ON THE CHURCH

data.question: Which are the attributes of the Church?
data.answer: The attributes of the Church are three: authority infallibility, and indefectibility.
data.group: LESSON TWELFTH: ON THE ATTRIBUTES AND MARKS OF THE CHURCH

data.question: What do you mean by the authority of the Church?
data.answer: By the authority of the Church I mean the right and power which the Pope and the bishops, as the successors of the Apostles, have to teach and to govern the faithful.
data.group: LESSON TWELFTH: ON THE ATTRIBUTES AND MARKS OF THE CHURCH

data.question: What do you mean by the infallibility of the Church?
data.answer: By the infallibility of the Church I mean that the Church cannot err when it teaches a doctrine of faith or morals.
data.group: LESSON TWELFTH: ON THE ATTRIBUTES AND MARKS OF THE CHURCH

data.question: When does the Church teach infallibly?
data.answer: The Church teaches infallibly when it speaks through the Pope and the bishops, united in general council, or through the Pope alone when he proclaims to all the faithful a doctrine of faith or morals.
data.group: LESSON TWELFTH: ON THE ATTRIBUTES AND MARKS OF THE CHURCH

data.question: What do you mean by the indefectibility of the Church?
data.answer: By the indefectibility of the Church I mean that the Church, as Christ founded it, will last till the end of time.
data.group: LESSON TWELFTH: ON THE ATTRIBUTES AND MARKS OF THE CHURCH

data.question: In whom are these attributes found in their fullness?
data.answer: These attributes are found in their fullness in the Pope, the visible Head of the Church, whose infallible authority to teach bishops, priests, and people in matters of faith or morals will last till the end of the world.
data.group: LESSON TWELFTH: ON THE ATTRIBUTES AND MARKS OF THE CHURCH

data.question: Has the Church any marks by which it may be known?
data.answer: The Church has four marks by which it may be known: it is One; it is Holy; it is Catholic; it is Apostolic.
data.group: LESSON TWELFTH: ON THE ATTRIBUTES AND MARKS OF THE CHURCH

data.question: How is the Church One?
data.answer: The Church is One because all its members agree in one faith, are all in one communion, and are all under one Head.
data.group: LESSON TWELFTH: ON THE ATTRIBUTES AND MARKS OF THE CHURCH

data.question: How is the Church Holy?
data.answer: The Church is Holy because its founder, Jesus Christ, is holy; because it teaches a holy doctrine; invites all to a holy life; and because of the eminent holiness of so many thousands of its children.
data.group: LESSON TWELFTH: ON THE ATTRIBUTES AND MARKS OF THE CHURCH

data.question: How is the Church Catholic or universal?
data.answer: The Church is Catholic or universal because it subsists in all ages, teaches all nations, and maintains all truth.
data.group: LESSON TWELFTH: ON THE ATTRIBUTES AND MARKS OF THE CHURCH

data.question: How is the Church Apostolic?
data.answer: The Church is Apostolic because it was founded by Christ on His Apostles, and is governed by their lawful successors, and because it has never ceased, and never will cease, to teach their doctrine.
data.group: LESSON TWELFTH: ON THE ATTRIBUTES AND MARKS OF THE CHURCH

data.question: In which Church are these attributes and marks found?
data.answer: These attributes and marks are found in the Holy Roman Catholic Church alone.
data.group: LESSON TWELFTH: ON THE ATTRIBUTES AND MARKS OF THE CHURCH

data.question: From whom does the Church derive its undying life and infallible authority?
data.answer: The Church derives its undying life and infallible authority from the Holy Ghost, the spirit of truth, who abides with it forever.
data.group: LESSON TWELFTH: ON THE ATTRIBUTES AND MARKS OF THE CHURCH

data.question: By whom is the Church made and kept One, Holy, and Catholic?
data.answer: The Church is made and kept One, Holy, and Catholic by the Holy Ghost, the spirit of love and holiness, who unites and sanctifies its members throughout the world.
data.group: LESSON TWELFTH: ON THE ATTRIBUTES AND MARKS OF THE CHURCH

data.question: What is a Sacrament?
data.answer: A Sacrament is an outward sign instituted by Christ to give grace.
data.group: LESSON THIRTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTS IN GENERAL

data.question: How many Sacraments are there?
data.answer: There are seven Sacraments: Baptism, Confirmation, Holy Eucharist, Penance, Extreme Unction, Holy Orders, and Matrimony.
data.group: LESSON THIRTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTS IN GENERAL

data.question: Whence have the Sacraments the power of giving grace?
data.answer: The Sacraments have the power of giving grace from the merits of Jesus Christ.
data.group: LESSON THIRTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTS IN GENERAL

data.question: What grace do the Sacraments give?
data.answer: Some of the Sacraments give sanctifying grace, and others increase it in our souls.
data.group: LESSON THIRTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTS IN GENERAL

data.question: Which are the Sacraments that give sanctifying grace?
data.answer: The Sacraments that give sanctifying grace are Baptism and Penance; and they are called Sacraments of the dead.
data.group: LESSON THIRTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTS IN GENERAL

data.question: Why are Baptism and Penance called Sacraments of the dead?
data.answer: Baptism and Penance are called Sacraments of the dead, because they take away sin, which is the death of the soul, and give grace, which is its life.
data.group: LESSON THIRTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTS IN GENERAL

data.question: Which are the Sacraments that increase sanctifying grace in our soul?
data.answer: The Sacraments that increase sanctifying grace in our soul are: Confirmation, Holy Eucharist, Extreme Unction, Holy Orders, and Matrimony; and they are called Sacraments of the living.
data.group: LESSON THIRTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTS IN GENERAL

data.question: Why are Confirmation, Holy Eucharist, Extreme Unction, Holy Orders, and Matrimony called Sacraments of the living?
data.answer: Confirmation, Holy Eucharist, Extreme Unction, Holy Orders, and Matrimony are called Sacraments of the living, because those who receive them worthily are already living the life of grace.
data.group: LESSON THIRTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTS IN GENERAL

data.question: What sin does he commit who receives the Sacraments of the living in mortal sin?
data.answer: He who receives the Sacraments of the living in mortal sin commits a sacrilege, which is a great sin, because it is an abuse of a sacred thing.
data.group: LESSON THIRTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTS IN GENERAL

data.question: Besides sanctifying grace do the Sacraments give any other grace?
data.answer: Besides sanctifying grace the Sacraments give another grace, called sacramental.
data.group: LESSON THIRTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTS IN GENERAL

data.question: What is sacramental grace?
data.answer: Sacramental grace is a special help which God gives, to attain the end for which He instituted each Sacrament.
data.group: LESSON THIRTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTS IN GENERAL

data.question: Do the Sacraments always give grace?
data.answer: The Sacraments always give grace, if we receive them with the right dispositions.
data.group: LESSON THIRTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTS IN GENERAL

data.question: Can we receive the Sacraments more than once?
data.answer: We can receive the Sacraments more than once, except Baptism, Confirmation, and Holy Orders.
data.group: LESSON THIRTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTS IN GENERAL

data.question: Why can we not receive Baptism, Confirmation, and Holy Orders more than once?
data.answer: We cannot receive Baptism, Confirmation, and Holy Orders more than once, because they imprint a character in the soul.
data.group: LESSON THIRTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTS IN GENERAL

data.question: What is the character which these Sacraments imprint in the soul?
data.answer: The character which these Sacraments imprint in the soul is a spiritual mark which remains forever.
data.group: LESSON THIRTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTS IN GENERAL

data.question: Does this character remain in the soul even after death?
data.answer: This character remains in the soul even after death: for the honor and glory of those who are saved; for the shame and punishment of those who are lost.
data.group: LESSON THIRTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTS IN GENERAL

data.question: What is Baptism?
data.answer: Baptism is a Sacrament which cleanses us from original sin, makes us Christians, children of God, and heirs of heaven.
data.group: LESSON FOURTEENTH: ON BAPTISM

data.question: Are actual sins ever remitted by Baptism?
data.answer: Actual sins and all the punishment due to them are remitted by Baptism, if the person baptized be guilty of any.
data.group: LESSON FOURTEENTH: ON BAPTISM

data.question: Is Baptism necessary to salvation?
data.answer: Baptism is necessary to salvation, because without it we cannot enter into the kingdom of heaven.
data.group: LESSON FOURTEENTH: ON BAPTISM

data.question: Who can administer Baptism?
data.answer: The priest is the ordinary minister of Baptism; but in case of necessity any one who has the use of reason may baptize.
data.group: LESSON FOURTEENTH: ON BAPTISM

data.question: How is Baptism given?
data.answer: Whoever baptizes should pour water on the head of the person to be baptized, and say, while pouring the water: I baptize thee in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost.
data.group: LESSON FOURTEENTH: ON BAPTISM

data.question: How many kinds of Baptism are there?
data.answer: There are three kinds of Baptism: Baptism of water, of desire, and of blood.
data.group: LESSON FOURTEENTH: ON BAPTISM

data.question: What is Baptism of water?
data.answer: Baptism of water is that which is given by pouring water on the head of the person to be baptized, and saying at the same time: I baptize thee in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost.
data.group: LESSON FOURTEENTH: ON BAPTISM

data.question: What is Baptism of desire?
data.answer: Baptism of desire is an ardent wish to receive Baptism, and to do all that God has ordained for our salvation.
data.group: LESSON FOURTEENTH: ON BAPTISM

data.question: What is Baptism of blood?
data.answer: Baptism of blood is the shedding of one's blood for the faith of Christ.
data.group: LESSON FOURTEENTH: ON BAPTISM

data.question: Is Baptism of desire or of blood sufficient to produce the effects of Baptism of water?
data.answer: Baptism of desire or of blood is sufficient to produce the effects of the Baptism of water, if it is impossible to receive the Baptism of water.
data.group: LESSON FOURTEENTH: ON BAPTISM

data.question: What do we promise in Baptism?
data.answer: In Baptism we promise to renounce the devil with all his works and pomps.
data.group: LESSON FOURTEENTH: ON BAPTISM

data.question: Why is the name of a saint given in Baptism?
data.answer: The name of a saint is given in Baptism in order that the person baptized may imitate his virtues and have him for a protector.
data.group: LESSON FOURTEENTH: ON BAPTISM

data.question: Why are godfathers and godmothers given in Baptism?
data.answer: Godfathers and godmothers are given in Baptism in order that they may promise, in the name of the child, what the child itself would promise if it had the use of reason.
data.group: LESSON FOURTEENTH: ON BAPTISM

data.question: What is the obligation of a godfather and a godmother?
data.answer: The obligation of a godfather and a godmother is to instruct the child in its religious duties, if the parents neglect to do so or die.
data.group: LESSON FOURTEENTH: ON BAPTISM

data.question: What is Confirmation?
data.answer: Confirmation is a Sacrament through which we receive the Holy Ghost to make us strong and perfect Christians and soldiers of Jesus Christ.
data.group: LESSON FIFTEENTH: ON CONFIRMATION

data.question: Who administers Confirmation?
data.answer: The bishop is the ordinary minister of Confirmation.
data.group: LESSON FIFTEENTH: ON CONFIRMATION

data.question: How does the bishop give Confirmation?
data.answer: The bishop extends his hands over those who are to be confirmed, prays that they may receive the Holy Ghost, and anoints the forehead of each with holy chrism in the form of a cross.
data.group: LESSON FIFTEENTH: ON CONFIRMATION

data.question: What is holy chrism?
data.answer: Holy chrism is a mixture of olive-oil and balm, consecrated by the bishop.
data.group: LESSON FIFTEENTH: ON CONFIRMATION

data.question: What does the bishop say in anointing the person he confirms?
data.answer: In anointing the person he confirms the bishop says: I sign thee with the sign of the cross, and I confirm thee with the chrism of salvation, in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost.
data.group: LESSON FIFTEENTH: ON CONFIRMATION

data.question: What is meant by anointing the forehead with chrism in the form of a cross?
data.answer: By anointing the forehead with chrism in the form of a cross is meant, that the Christian who is confirmed must openly profess and practice his faith, never be ashamed of it, and rather die than deny it.
data.group: LESSON FIFTEENTH: ON CONFIRMATION

data.question: Why does the bishop give the person he confirms a slight blow on the cheek?
data.answer: The bishop gives the person he confirms a slight blow on the cheek, to put him in mind that he must be ready to suffer everything, even death, for the sake of Christ.
data.group: LESSON FIFTEENTH: ON CONFIRMATION

data.question: To receive Confirmation worthily is it necessary to be in the state of grace?
data.answer: To receive Confirmation worthily it is necessary to be in the state of grace.
data.group: LESSON FIFTEENTH: ON CONFIRMATION

data.question: What special preparation should be made to receive Confirmation?
data.answer: Persons of an age to learn should know the chief mysteries of faith and the duties of a Christian, and be instructed in the nature and effects of this Sacrament.
data.group: LESSON FIFTEENTH: ON CONFIRMATION

data.question: Is it a sin to neglect Confirmation?
data.answer: It is a sin to neglect Confirmation, especially in these evil days when faith and morals are exposed to so many and such violent temptations.
data.group: LESSON FIFTEENTH: ON CONFIRMATION

data.question: Which are the effects of Confirmation?
data.answer: The effects of Confirmation are an increase of sanctifying grace, the strengthening of our faith, and the gifts of the Holy Ghost.
data.group: LESSON SIXTEENTH: ON THE GIFTS AND FRUITS OF THE HOLY GHOST

data.question: Which are the gifts of the Holy Ghost?
data.answer: The gifts of the Holy Ghost are Wisdom, Understanding, Counsel, Fortitude, Knowledge, Piety and Fear of the Lord.
data.group: LESSON SIXTEENTH: ON THE GIFTS AND FRUITS OF THE HOLY GHOST

data.question: Why do we receive the gift of Fear of the Lord?
data.answer: We receive the gift of Fear of the Lord to fill us with a dread of sin.
data.group: LESSON SIXTEENTH: ON THE GIFTS AND FRUITS OF THE HOLY GHOST

data.question: Why do we receive the gift of Piety?
data.answer: We receive the gift of Piety to make us love God as a Father and obey Him because we love Him.
data.group: LESSON SIXTEENTH: ON THE GIFTS AND FRUITS OF THE HOLY GHOST

data.question: Why do we receive the gift of Knowledge?
data.answer: We receive the gift of Knowledge to enable us to discover the will of God in all things.
data.group: LESSON SIXTEENTH: ON THE GIFTS AND FRUITS OF THE HOLY GHOST

data.question: Why do we receive the gift of Fortitude?
data.answer: We receive the gift of Fortitude to strengthen us to do the will of God in all things.
data.group: LESSON SIXTEENTH: ON THE GIFTS AND FRUITS OF THE HOLY GHOST

data.question: Why do we receive the gift of Counsel?
data.answer: We receive the gift of Counsel to warn us of the deceits of the devil, and of the dangers to salvation.
data.group: LESSON SIXTEENTH: ON THE GIFTS AND FRUITS OF THE HOLY GHOST

data.question: Why do we receive the gift of Understanding?
data.answer: We receive the gift of Understanding to enable us to know more clearly the mysteries of faith.
data.group: LESSON SIXTEENTH: ON THE GIFTS AND FRUITS OF THE HOLY GHOST

data.question: Why do we receive the gift of Wisdom?
data.answer: We receive the gift of Wisdom to give us a relish for the things of God, and to direct our whole life and all our actions to His honor and glory.
data.group: LESSON SIXTEENTH: ON THE GIFTS AND FRUITS OF THE HOLY GHOST

data.question: Which are the Beatitudes?
data.answer: The Beatitudes are:
data.group: LESSON SIXTEENTH: ON THE GIFTS AND FRUITS OF THE HOLY GHOST

data.question: Which are the twelve fruits of the Holy Ghost?
data.answer: The twelve fruits of the Holy Ghost are Charity, Joy, Peace, Patience, Benignity, Goodness, Long-suffering, Mildness, Faith, Modesty, Continency, and Chastity.
data.group: LESSON SIXTEENTH: ON THE GIFTS AND FRUITS OF THE HOLY GHOST

data.question: What is the Sacrament of Penance?
data.answer: Penance is a Sacrament in which the sins committed after Baptism are forgiven.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENT OF PENANCE

data.question: How does the Sacrament of Penance remit sin, and restore to the soul the friendship of God?
data.answer: The Sacrament of Penance remits sins and restores the friendship of God to the soul by means of the absolution of the priest.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENT OF PENANCE

data.question: How do you know that the priest has the power of absolving from the sins committed after Baptism?
data.answer: I know that the priest has the power of absolving from the sins committed after Baptism, because Jesus Christ granted that power to the priests of His Church when He said: "Receive ye the Holy Ghost. Whose sins you shall forgive, they are forgiven them; whose sins you shall retain, they are retained."
data.group: LESSON SEVENTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENT OF PENANCE

data.question: How do the priests of the Church exercise the power of forgiving sins?
data.answer: The priests of the Church exercise the power of forgiving sins by hearing the confession of sins, and granting pardon for them as ministers of God and in His name.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENT OF PENANCE

data.question: What must we do to receive the Sacrament of Penance worthily?
data.answer: To receive the Sacrament of Penance worthily we must do five things:
data.group: LESSON SEVENTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENT OF PENANCE

data.question: What is the examination of conscience?
data.answer: The examination of conscience is an earnest effort to recall to mind all the sins we have committed since our last worthy confession.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENT OF PENANCE

data.question: How can we make a good examination of conscience?
data.answer: We can make a good examination of conscience by calling to memory the commandments of God, the precepts of the Church, the seven capital sins, and the particular duties of our state in life, to find out the sins we have committed.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENT OF PENANCE

data.question: What should we do before beginning the examination of conscience?
data.answer: Before beginning the examination of conscience we should pray to God to give us light to know our sins and grace to detest them.
data.group: LESSON SEVENTEENTH: ON THE SACRAMENT OF PENANCE

data.question: What is contrition, or sorrow for sin?
data.answer: Contrition, or sorrow for sin, is a hatred of sin and a true grief of the soul for having offended God, with a firm purpose of sinning no more.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTEENTH: ON CONTRITION

data.question: What kind of sorrow should we have for our sins?
data.answer: The sorrow we should have for our sins should be interior, supernatural, universal, and sovereign.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTEENTH: ON CONTRITION

data.question: What do you mean by saying that our sorrow should be interior?
data.answer: When I say that our sorrow should be interior, I mean that it should come from the heart, and not merely from the lips.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTEENTH: ON CONTRITION

data.question: What do you mean by saying that our sorrow should be supernatural?
data.answer: When I say that our sorrow should be supernatural, I mean that it should be prompted by the grace of God, and excited by motives which spring from faith, and not by merely natural motives.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTEENTH: ON CONTRITION

data.question: What do you mean by saying that our sorrow should be universal?
data.answer: When I say that our sorrow should be universal, I mean that we should be sorry for all our mortal sins without exception.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTEENTH: ON CONTRITION

data.question: What do you mean when you say that our sorrow should be sovereign?
data.answer: When I say that our sorrow should be sovereign, I mean that we should grieve more for having offended God than for any other evil that can befall us.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTEENTH: ON CONTRITION

data.question: Why should we be sorry for our sins?
data.answer: We should be sorry for our sins, because sin is the greatest of evils and an offense against God our Creator, Preserver, and Redeemer, and because it shuts us out of heaven and condemns us to the eternal pains of hell.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTEENTH: ON CONTRITION

data.question: How many kinds of contrition are there?
data.answer: There are two kinds of contrition: perfect contrition and imperfect contrition.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTEENTH: ON CONTRITION

data.question: What is perfect contrition?
data.answer: Perfect contrition is that which fills us with sorrow and hatred for sin, because it offends God, who is infinitely good in Himself and worthy of all love.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTEENTH: ON CONTRITION

data.question: What is imperfect contrition?
data.answer: Imperfect contrition is that by which we hate what offends God, because by it we lose heaven and deserve hell; or because sin is so hateful in itself.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTEENTH: ON CONTRITION

data.question: Is imperfect contrition sufficient for a worthy confession?
data.answer: Imperfect contrition is sufficient for a worthy confession, but we should endeavor to have perfect contrition.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTEENTH: ON CONTRITION

data.question: What do you mean by a firm purpose of sinning no more?
data.answer: By a firm purpose of sinning no more I mean a fixed resolve not only to avoid all mortal sin, but also its near occasions.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTEENTH: ON CONTRITION

data.question: What do you mean by the near occasions of sin?
data.answer: By the near occasions of sin I mean all the persons, places, and things that may easily lead us into sin.
data.group: LESSON EIGHTEENTH: ON CONTRITION

data.question: What is Confession?
data.answer: Confession is the telling of our sins to a duly authorized priest, for the purpose of obtaining forgiveness.
data.group: LESSON NINETEENTH: ON CONFESSION

data.question: What sins are we bound to confess?
data.answer: We are bound to confess all our mortal sins, but it is well also to confess our venial sins.
data.group: LESSON NINETEENTH: ON CONFESSION

data.question: Which are the chief qualities of a good Confession?
data.answer: The chief qualities of a good Confession are three: it must be humble, sincere, and entire.
data.group: LESSON NINETEENTH: ON CONFESSION

data.question: When is our Confession humble?
data.answer: Our Confession is humble, when we accuse our selves of our sins, with a deep sense of shame and sorrow for having offended God.
data.group: LESSON NINETEENTH: ON CONFESSION

data.question: When is our Confession sincere?
data.answer: Our Confession is sincere, when we tell our sins honestly and truthfully, neither exaggerating nor excusing them.
data.group: LESSON NINETEENTH: ON CONFESSION

data.question: When is our Confession entire?
data.answer: Our Confession is entire, when we tell the number and kinds of our sins and the circumstances which change their nature.
data.group: LESSON NINETEENTH: ON CONFESSION

data.question: What should we do if we cannot remember the number of our sins?
data.answer: If we cannot remember the number of our sins, we should tell the number as nearly as possible, and say how often we may have sinned in a day, a week, or a month, and how long the habit or practice has lasted.
data.group: LESSON NINETEENTH: ON CONFESSION

data.question: Is our Confession worthy if, without our fault, we forget to confess a mortal sin?
data.answer: If without our fault we forget to confess a mortal sin, our Confession is worthy, and the sin is forgiven; but it must be told in Confession if it again comes to our mind.
data.group: LESSON NINETEENTH: ON CONFESSION

data.question: Is it a grievous offense wilfully to conceal a mortal sin in Confession?
data.answer: It is a grievous offense wilfully to conceal a mortal sin in Confession, because we thereby tell a lie to the Holy Ghost, and make our Confession worthless.
data.group: LESSON NINETEENTH: ON CONFESSION

data.question: What must he do who has wilfully concealed a mortal sin in Confession?
data.answer: He who has wilfully concealed a mortal sin in Confession must not only confess it, but must also repeat all the sins he has committed since his last worthy Confession.
data.group: LESSON NINETEENTH: ON CONFESSION

data.question: Why does the priest give us a penance after Confession?
data.answer: The priest gives us a penance after Confession, that we may satisfy God for the temporal punishment due to our sins.
data.group: LESSON NINETEENTH: ON CONFESSION

data.question: Does not the Sacrament of Penance remit all punishment due to sin?
data.answer: The Sacrament of Penance remits the eternal punishment due to sin, but it does not always remit the temporal punishment which God requires as satisfaction for our sins.
data.group: LESSON NINETEENTH: ON CONFESSION

data.question: Why does God require a temporal punishment as a satisfaction for sin?
data.answer: God requires a temporal punishment as a satisfaction for sin, to teach us the great evil of sin and to prevent us from falling again.
data.group: LESSON NINETEENTH: ON CONFESSION

data.question: Which are the chief means by which we satisfy God for the temporal punishment due to sin?
data.answer: The chief means by which we satisfy God for the temporal punishment due to sin are: Prayer, Fasting, Almsgiving, all spiritual and corporal works of mercy, and the patient suffering of the ills of life.
data.group: LESSON NINETEENTH: ON CONFESSION

data.question: Which are the chief spiritual works of mercy?
data.answer: The chief spiritual works of mercy are seven: To admonish the sinner, to instruct the ignorant, to counsel the doubtful, to comfort the sorrowful, to bear wrongs patiently, to forgive all injuries, and to pray for the living and the dead.
data.group: LESSON NINETEENTH: ON CONFESSION

data.question: Which are the chief corporal works of mercy?
data.answer: The chief corporal works of mercy are seven: To feed the hungry, to give drink to the thirsty, to clothe the naked, to ransom the captive, to harbor the harborless, to visit the sick, and to bury the dead.
data.group: LESSON NINETEENTH: ON CONFESSION

data.question: What should we do on entering the confessional?
data.answer: On entering the confessional we should kneel, make the sign of the Cross, and say to the priest, Bless me, Father; then add, I confess to Almighty God and to you, Father, that I have sinned.
data.group: LESSON TWENTIETH: ON THE MANNER OF MAKING A GOOD CONFESSION

data.question: Which are the first things we should tell the priest in Confession?
data.answer: The first things we should tell the priest in Confession are the time of our last Confession, and whether we said the penance and went to Holy Communion.
data.group: LESSON TWENTIETH: ON THE MANNER OF MAKING A GOOD CONFESSION

data.question: After telling the time of our last Confession and Communion what should we do?
data.answer: After telling the time of our last Confession and Communion we should confess all the mortal sins we have since committed, and all the venial sins we may wish to mention.
data.group: LESSON TWENTIETH: ON THE MANNER OF MAKING A GOOD CONFESSION

data.question: What must we do when the confessor asks us questions?
data.answer: When the confessor asks us questions we must answer them truthfully and clearly.
data.group: LESSON TWENTIETH: ON THE MANNER OF MAKING A GOOD CONFESSION

data.question: What should we do after telling our sins?
data.answer: After telling our sins we should listen with attention to the advice which the confessor may think proper to give.
data.group: LESSON TWENTIETH: ON THE MANNER OF MAKING A GOOD CONFESSION

data.question: How should we end our Confession?
data.answer: We should end our Confession by saying, I also accuse myself of all the sins of my past life, telling, if we choose, one or several of our past sins.
data.group: LESSON TWENTIETH: ON THE MANNER OF MAKING A GOOD CONFESSION

data.question: What should we do while the priest is giving us absolution?
data.answer: While the priest is giving us absolution we should from our heart renew the Act of Contrition.
data.group: LESSON TWENTIETH: ON THE MANNER OF MAKING A GOOD CONFESSION

data.question: What is an Indulgence?
data.answer: An Indulgence is the remission in whole or in part of the temporal punishment due to sin.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FIRST: ON INDULGENCES

data.question: Is an Indulgence a pardon of sin, or a license to commit sin?
data.answer: An Indulgence is not a pardon of sin, nor a license to commit sin, and one who is in a state of mortal sin cannot gain an Indulgence.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FIRST: ON INDULGENCES

data.question: How many kinds of Indulgences are there?
data.answer: There are two kinds of Indulgences--Plenary and Partial.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FIRST: ON INDULGENCES

data.question: What is a Plenary Indulgence?
data.answer: A Plenary Indulgence is the full remission of the temporal punishment due to sin.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FIRST: ON INDULGENCES

data.question: What is a Partial Indulgence?
data.answer: A Partial Indulgence is the remission of a part of the temporal punishment due to sin.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FIRST: ON INDULGENCES

data.question: How does the Church by means of Indulgences remit the temporal punishment due to sin?
data.answer: The Church by means of Indulgences remits the temporal punishment due to sin by applying to us the merits of Jesus Christ, and the superabundant satisfactions of the Blessed Virgin Mary and of the saints; which merits and satisfactions are its spiritual treasury.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FIRST: ON INDULGENCES

data.question: What must we do to gain an Indulgence?
data.answer: To gain an Indulgence we must be in the state of grace and perform the works enjoined.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FIRST: ON INDULGENCES

data.question: What is the Holy Eucharist?
data.answer: The Holy Eucharist is the Sacrament which contains the body and blood, soul and divinity, of our Lord Jesus Christ under the appearances of bread and wine.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SECOND: ON THE HOLY EUCHARIST

data.question: When did Christ institute the Holy Eucharist?
data.answer: Christ instituted the Holy Eucharist at the Last Supper, the night before He died.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SECOND: ON THE HOLY EUCHARIST

data.question: Who were present when our Lord instituted the Holy Eucharist?
data.answer: When our Lord instituted the Holy Eucharist the twelve Apostles were present.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SECOND: ON THE HOLY EUCHARIST

data.question: How did our Lord institute the Holy Eucharist?
data.answer: Our Lord instituted the Holy Eucharist by taking bread, blessing, breaking, and giving to His Apostles, saying: Take ye and eat. This is My body; and then by taking the cup of wine, blessing and giving it, saying to them: Drink ye all of this. This is My blood which shall be shed for the remission of sins. Do this for a commemoration of Me.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SECOND: ON THE HOLY EUCHARIST

data.question: What happened when our Lord said, This is My body; this is My blood?
data.answer: When our Lord said, This is My body, the substance of the bread was changed into the substance of His body; when He said, This is My blood, the substance of the wine was changed into the substance of His blood.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SECOND: ON THE HOLY EUCHARIST

data.question: Is Jesus Christ whole and entire both under the form of bread and under the form of wine?
data.answer: Jesus Christ is whole and entire both under the form of bread and under the form of wine.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SECOND: ON THE HOLY EUCHARIST

data.question: Did anything remain of the bread and wine after their substance had been changed into the substance of the body and blood of our Lord?
data.answer: After the substance of the bread and wine had been changed into the substance of the body and blood of our Lord there remained only the appearances of bread and wine.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SECOND: ON THE HOLY EUCHARIST

data.question: What do you mean by the appearances of bread and wine?
data.answer: By the appearances of bread and wine I mean the figure, the color, the taste, and whatever appears to the senses.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SECOND: ON THE HOLY EUCHARIST

data.question: What is this change of the bread and wine into the body and blood of our Lord called?
data.answer: This change of the bread and wine into the body and blood of our Lord is called Transubstantiation.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SECOND: ON THE HOLY EUCHARIST

data.question: How was the substance of the bread and wine changed into the substance of the body and blood of Christ?
data.answer: The substance of the bread and wine was changed into the substance of the body and blood of Christ by His almighty power.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SECOND: ON THE HOLY EUCHARIST

data.question: Does this change of bread and wine into the body and blood of Christ continue to be made in the Church?
data.answer: This change of bread and wine into the body and blood of Christ continues to be made in the Church by Jesus Christ through the ministry of His priests.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SECOND: ON THE HOLY EUCHARIST

data.question: When did Christ give His priests the power to change bread and wine into His body and blood?
data.answer: Christ gave His priests the power to change bread and wine into His body and blood when He said to the Apostles, Do this in commemoration of Me.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SECOND: ON THE HOLY EUCHARIST

data.question: How do the priests exercise this power of changing bread and wine into the body and blood of Christ?
data.answer: The priests exercise this power of changing bread and wine into the body and blood of Christ through the words of consecration in the Mass, which are the words of Christ: This is My body; this is My blood.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SECOND: ON THE HOLY EUCHARIST

data.question: Why did Christ institute the Holy Eucharist?
data.answer: Christ instituted the Holy Eucharist--
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-THIRD: ON THE ENDS FOR WHICH THE HOLY EUCHARIST WAS INSTITUTED

data.question: How are we united to Jesus Christ in the Holy Eucharist?
data.answer: We are united to Jesus Christ in the Holy Eucharist by means of Holy Communion.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-THIRD: ON THE ENDS FOR WHICH THE HOLY EUCHARIST WAS INSTITUTED

data.question: What is Holy Communion?
data.answer: Holy Communion is the receiving of the body and blood of Christ.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-THIRD: ON THE ENDS FOR WHICH THE HOLY EUCHARIST WAS INSTITUTED

data.question: What is necessary to make a good Communion?
data.answer: To make a good Communion it is necessary to be in the state of sanctifying grace, to have a right intention, and to obey the laws of fasting. (See Q. 257.)
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-THIRD: ON THE ENDS FOR WHICH THE HOLY EUCHARIST WAS INSTITUTED

data.question: Does he who receives Communion in mortal sin receive the body and blood of Christ?
data.answer: He who receives Communion in mortal sin receives the body and blood of Christ, but does not receive His grace, and he commits a great sacrilege.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-THIRD: ON THE ENDS FOR WHICH THE HOLY EUCHARIST WAS INSTITUTED

data.question: Is it enough to be free from mortal sin to receive plentifully the graces of Holy Communion?
data.answer: To receive plentifully the graces of Holy Communion it is not enough to be free from mortal sin, but we should be free from all affection to venial sin, and should make acts of faith, hope, and love.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-THIRD: ON THE ENDS FOR WHICH THE HOLY EUCHARIST WAS INSTITUTED

data.question: What is the fast necessary for Holy Communion?
data.answer: The fast necessary for Holy Communion is to abstain from all food, beverages, and alcoholic drinks for one hour before Holy Communion. Water may be taken at any time. The sick may take food, non-alcoholic drinks, and any medicine up to Communion time.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-THIRD: ON THE ENDS FOR WHICH THE HOLY EUCHARIST WAS INSTITUTED

data.question: Is any one ever allowed to receive Holy Communion when not fasting?
data.answer: Any one in danger of death is allowed to receive Holy Communion when not fasting or when it is necessary to save the Blessed Sacrament from insult or injury.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-THIRD: ON THE ENDS FOR WHICH THE HOLY EUCHARIST WAS INSTITUTED

data.question: When are we bound to receive Holy Communion?
data.answer: We are bound to receive Holy Communion, under pain of mortal sin, during the Easter time and when in danger of death.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-THIRD: ON THE ENDS FOR WHICH THE HOLY EUCHARIST WAS INSTITUTED

data.question: Is it well to receive Holy Communion often?
data.answer: It is well to receive Holy Communion often, as nothing is a greater aid to a holy life than often to receive the Author of all grace and the Source of all good.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-THIRD: ON THE ENDS FOR WHICH THE HOLY EUCHARIST WAS INSTITUTED

data.question: What should we do after Holy Communion?
data.answer: After Holy Communion we should spend some time in adoring our Lord, in thanking Him for the grace we have received, and in asking Him for the blessings we need.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-THIRD: ON THE ENDS FOR WHICH THE HOLY EUCHARIST WAS INSTITUTED

data.question: When and where are the bread and wine changed into the body and blood of Christ?
data.answer: The bread and wine are changed into the body and blood of Christ at the Consecration in the Mass.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FOURTH: ON THE SACRIFICE OF THE MASS

data.question: What is the Mass?
data.answer: The Mass is the unbloody sacrifice of the body and blood of Christ.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FOURTH: ON THE SACRIFICE OF THE MASS

data.question: What is a sacrifice?
data.answer: A sacrifice is the offering of an object by a priest to God alone, and the consuming of it to acknowledge that He is the Creator and Lord of all things.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FOURTH: ON THE SACRIFICE OF THE MASS

data.question: Is the Mass the same sacrifice as that of the Cross?
data.answer: The Mass is the same sacrifice as that of the Cross.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FOURTH: ON THE SACRIFICE OF THE MASS

data.question: How is the Mass the same sacrifice as that of the Cross?
data.answer: The Mass is the same sacrifice as that of the Cross because the offering and the priest are the same--Christ our Blessed Lord; and the ends for which the sacrifice of the Mass is offered are the same as those of the sacrifice of the Cross.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FOURTH: ON THE SACRIFICE OF THE MASS

data.question: What were the ends for which the sacrifice of the Cross was offered?
data.answer: The ends for which the sacrifice of the Cross was offered were: 1st, To honor and glorify God; 2d, To thank Him for all the graces bestowed on the whole world; 3d, To satisfy God's justice for the sins of men; 4th, To obtain all graces and blessings.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FOURTH: ON THE SACRIFICE OF THE MASS

data.question: Is there any difference between the sacrifice of the Cross and the sacrifice of the Mass?
data.answer: Yes; the manner in which the sacrifice is offered is different. On the Cross Christ really shed His blood and was really slain; in the Mass there is no real shedding of blood nor real death, because Christ can die no more; but the sacrifice of the Mass, through the separate consecration of the bread and the wine, represents His death on the Cross.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FOURTH: ON THE SACRIFICE OF THE MASS

data.question: How should we assist at Mass?
data.answer: We should assist at Mass with great interior recollection and piety and with every outward mark of respect and devotion.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FOURTH: ON THE SACRIFICE OF THE MASS

data.question: Which is the best manner of hearing Mass?
data.answer: The best manner of hearing Mass is to offer it to God with the priest for the same purpose for which it is said, to meditate on Christ's sufferings and death, and to go to Holy Communion.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FOURTH: ON THE SACRIFICE OF THE MASS

data.question: What is the Sacrament of Extreme Unction?
data.answer: Extreme Unction is the Sacrament which, through the anointing and prayer of the priest, gives health and strength to the soul, and sometimes to the body, when we are in danger of death from sickness.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FIFTH: ON EXTREME UNCTION AND HOLY ORDERS

data.question: When should we receive Extreme Unction?
data.answer: We should receive Extreme Unction when we are in danger of death from sickness, or from a wound or accident.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FIFTH: ON EXTREME UNCTION AND HOLY ORDERS

data.question: Should we wait until we are in extreme danger before we receive Extreme Unction?
data.answer: We should not wait until we are in extreme danger before we receive Extreme Unction, but if possible we should receive it whilst we have the use of our senses.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FIFTH: ON EXTREME UNCTION AND HOLY ORDERS

data.question: Which are the effects of the Sacrament of Extreme Unction?
data.answer: The effects of Extreme Unction are: 1st, To comfort us in the pains of sickness and to strengthen us against temptation; 2d, To remit venial sins and to cleanse our soul from the remains of sin; 3d, To restore us to health, when God sees fit.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FIFTH: ON EXTREME UNCTION AND HOLY ORDERS

data.question: What do you mean by the remains of sin?
data.answer: By the remains of sin I mean the inclination to evil and the weakness of the will which are the result of our sins, and which remain after our sins have been forgiven.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FIFTH: ON EXTREME UNCTION AND HOLY ORDERS

data.question: How should we receive the Sacrament of Extreme Unction?
data.answer: We should receive the Sacrament of Extreme Unction in the state of grace, and with lively faith and resignation to the will of God.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FIFTH: ON EXTREME UNCTION AND HOLY ORDERS

data.question: Who is the minister of the Sacrament of Extreme Unction?
data.answer: The priest is the minister of the Sacrament of Extreme Unction.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FIFTH: ON EXTREME UNCTION AND HOLY ORDERS

data.question: What is the Sacrament of Holy Orders?
data.answer: Holy Orders is a Sacrament by which bishops, priests, and other ministers of the Church are ordained and receive the power and grace to perform their sacred duties.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FIFTH: ON EXTREME UNCTION AND HOLY ORDERS

data.question: What is necessary to receive Holy Orders worthily?
data.answer: To receive Holy Orders worthily it is necessary to be in the state of grace, to have the necessary knowledge and a divine call to this sacred office.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FIFTH: ON EXTREME UNCTION AND HOLY ORDERS

data.question: How should Christians look upon the priests of the Church?
data.answer: Christians should look upon the priests of the Church as the messengers of God and the dispensers of His mysteries.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FIFTH: ON EXTREME UNCTION AND HOLY ORDERS

data.question: Who can confer the Sacrament of Holy Orders?
data.answer: Bishops can confer the Sacrament of Holy Orders.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-FIFTH: ON EXTREME UNCTION AND HOLY ORDERS

data.question: What is the Sacrament of Matrimony?
data.answer: The Sacrament of Matrimony is the Sacrament which unites a Christian man and woman in lawful marriage.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SIXTH: ON MATRIMONY

data.question: Can a Christian man and woman be united in lawful marriage in any other way than by the Sacrament of Matrimony?
data.answer: A Christian man and woman cannot be united in lawful marriage in any other way than by the Sacrament of Matrimony, because Christ raised marriage to the dignity of a Sacrament.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SIXTH: ON MATRIMONY

data.question: Can the bond of Christian marriage be dissolved by any human power?
data.answer: The bond of Christian marriage cannot be dissolved by any human power.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SIXTH: ON MATRIMONY

data.question: Which are the effects of the Sacrament of Matrimony?
data.answer: The effects of the Sacrament of Matrimony are: 1st, To sanctify the love of husband and wife; 2d, To give them grace to bear with each other's weaknesses; 3d, To enable them to bring up their children in the fear and love of God.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SIXTH: ON MATRIMONY

data.question: To receive the Sacrament of matrimony worthily is it necessary to be in the state of grace?
data.answer: To receive the Sacrament of Matrimony worthily it is necessary to be in the state of grace, and it is necessary also to comply with the laws of the Church.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SIXTH: ON MATRIMONY

data.question: Who has the right to make laws concerning the Sacrament of marriage?
data.answer: The Church alone has the right to make laws concerning the Sacrament of marriage, though the state also has the right to make laws concerning the civil effects of the marriage contract.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SIXTH: ON MATRIMONY

data.question: Does the Church forbid the marriage of Catholics with persons who have a different religion or no religion at all?
data.answer: The Church does forbid the marriage of Catholics with persons who have a different religion or no religion at all.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SIXTH: ON MATRIMONY

data.question: Why does the Church forbid the marriage of Catholics with persons who have a different religion or no religion at all?
data.answer: The Church forbids the marriage of Catholics with persons who have a different religion or no religion at all, because such marriages generally lead to indifference, loss of faith, and to the neglect of the religious education of the children.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SIXTH: ON MATRIMONY

data.question: Why do many marriages prove unhappy?
data.answer: Many marriages prove unhappy because they are entered into hastily and without worthy motives.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SIXTH: ON MATRIMONY

data.question: How should Christians prepare for a holy and happy marriage?
data.answer: Christians should prepare for a holy and happy marriage by receiving the Sacraments of Penance and Holy Eucharist; by begging God to grant them a pure intention and to direct their choice; and by seeking the advice of their parents and the blessing of their pastors.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SIXTH: ON MATRIMONY

data.question: What is a sacramental?
data.answer: A sacramental is anything set apart or blessed by the Church to excite good thoughts and to increase devotion, and through these movements of the heart to remit venial sin.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SEVENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTALS

data.question: What is the difference between the Sacraments and the sacramentals?
data.answer: The difference between the Sacraments and the sacramentals is: 1st, The Sacraments were instituted by Jesus Christ and the sacramentals were instituted by the Church; 2d, The Sacraments give grace of themselves when we place no obstacle in the way; the sacramentals excite in us pious dispositions, by means of which we may obtain grace.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SEVENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTALS

data.question: Which is the chief sacramental used in the Church?
data.answer: The chief sacramental used in the Church is the sign of the Cross.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SEVENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTALS

data.question: How do we make the sign of the Cross?
data.answer: We make the sign of the Cross by putting the right hand to the forehead, then on the breast, and then to the left and right shoulders, saying, In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost. Amen.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SEVENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTALS

data.question: Why do we make the sign of the Cross?
data.answer: We make the sign of the Cross to show that we are Christians and to profess our belief in the chief mysteries of our religion.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SEVENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTALS

data.question: How is the sign of the Cross a profession of faith in the chief mysteries of our religion?
data.answer: The sign of the Cross is a profession of faith in the chief mysteries of our religion because it expresses the mysteries of the Unity and Trinity of God and of the Incarnation and death of our Lord.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SEVENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTALS

data.question: How does the sign of the Cross express the mystery of the Unity and Trinity of God?
data.answer: The words, In the name, express the Unity of God; the words that follow, of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost, express the mystery of the Trinity.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SEVENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTALS

data.question: How does the sign of the Cross express the mystery of the Incarnation and death of our Lord?
data.answer: The sign of the Cross expresses the mystery of the Incarnation by reminding us that the Son of God, having become man, suffered death on the cross.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SEVENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTALS

data.question: What other sacramental is in very frequent use?
data.answer: Another sacramental in very frequent use is holy water.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SEVENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTALS

data.question: What is holy water?
data.answer: Holy water is water blessed by the priest with solemn prayer to beg God's blessing on those who use it, and protection from the powers of darkness.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SEVENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTALS

data.question: Are there other sacramentals besides the sign of the Cross and holy water?
data.answer: Besides the sign of the Cross and holy water there are many other sacramentals, such as blessed candles, ashes, palms, crucifixes, images of the Blessed Virgin and of the saints, rosaries, and scapulars.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-SEVENTH: ON THE SACRAMENTALS

data.question: Is there any other means of obtaining God's grace than the Sacraments?
data.answer: There is another means of obtaining God's grace, and it is prayer.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-EIGHTH: ON PRAYER

data.question: What is prayer?
data.answer: Prayer is the lifting up of our minds and hearts to God to adore Him, to thank Him for His benefits, to ask His forgiveness, and to beg of Him all the graces we need whether for soul or body.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-EIGHTH: ON PRAYER

data.question: Is prayer necessary to salvation?
data.answer: Prayer is necessary to salvation, and without it no one having the use of reason can be saved.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-EIGHTH: ON PRAYER

data.question: At what particular times should we pray?
