data.answer: We should pray particularly on Sundays and holydays, every morning and night, in all dangers, temptations, and afflictions.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-EIGHTH: ON PRAYER

data.question: How should we pray?
data.answer: We should pray: 1st, With attention; 2d, With a sense of our own helplessness and dependence upon God; 3d, With a great desire for the graces we beg of God; 4th, With trust in God's goodness; 5th, With perseverance.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-EIGHTH: ON PRAYER

data.question: Which are the prayers most recommended to us?
data.answer: The prayers most recommended to us are the Lord's Prayer, the Hail Mary, the Apostles' Creed, the Confiteor, and the Acts of Faith, Hope, Love, and Contrition.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-EIGHTH: ON PRAYER

data.question: Are prayers said with distractions of any avail?
data.answer: Prayers said with wilful distractions are of no avail.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-EIGHTH: ON PRAYER

data.question: Is it enough to belong to God's Church in order to be saved?
data.answer: It is not enough to belong to the Church in order to be saved, but we must also keep the Commandments of God and of the Church.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Which are the Commandments that contain the whole law of God?
data.answer: The Commandments which contain the whole law of God are these two: 1st, Thou shalt love the Lord thy God with thy whole heart, with thy whole soul, with thy whole strength, and with thy whole mind; 2d, Thou shalt love thy neighbor as thyself.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Why do these two Commandments of the love of God and of our neighbor contain the whole law of God?
data.answer: These two Commandments of the love of God and of our neighbor contain the whole law of God because all the other Commandments are given either to help us to keep these two, or to direct us how to shun what is opposed to them.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Which are the Commandments of God?
data.answer: The Commandments of God are these ten.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Who gave the Ten Commandments?
data.answer: God Himself gave the Ten Commandments to Moses on Mount Sinai, and Christ our Lord confirmed them.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is the first Commandment?
data.answer: The first Commandment is: I am the Lord thy God: thou shalt not have strange gods before Me.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: How does the first Commandment help us to keep the great Commandment of the love of God?
data.answer: The first Commandment helps us to keep the great Commandment of the love of God because it commands us to adore God alone.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: How do we adore God?
data.answer: We adore God by faith, hope, and charity, by prayer and sacrifice.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: How may the first Commandment be broken?
data.answer: The first Commandment may be broken by giving to a creature the honor which belongs to God alone; by false worship; and by attributing to a creature a perfection which belongs to God alone.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Do those who make use of spells and charms, or who believe in dreams, in mediums, spiritists, fortune-tellers, and the like, sin against the first Commandment?
data.answer: Those who make use of spells and charms, or who believe in dreams, in mediums, spiritists, fortunetellers and the like, sin against the first Commandment, because they attribute to creatures perfections which belong to God alone.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Are sins against faith, hope and charity also sins against the first Commandment?
data.answer: Sins against faith, hope, and charity are also sins against the first Commandment.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: How does a person sin against faith?
data.answer: A person sins against faith: 1st, by not trying to know what God has taught; 2d, by refusing to believe all that God has taught; 3d, by neglecting to profess his belief in what God has taught.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: How do we fail to try to know what God has taught?
data.answer: We fail to try to know what God has taught by neglecting to learn the Christian doctrine.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Who are they who do not believe all that God has taught?
data.answer: They who do not believe all that God has taught are the heretics and infidels.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Who are they who neglect to profess their belief in what God has taught?
data.answer: They who neglect to profess their belief in what God has taught are all those who fail to acknowledge the true Church in which they really believe.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Can they who fail to profess their faith in the true Church in which they believe expect to be saved while in that state?
data.answer: They who fail to profess their faith in the true Church in which they believe cannot expect to be saved while in that state, for Christ has said: "Whoever shall deny Me before men, I will also deny him before My Father who is in heaven."
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Are we obliged to make open profession of our faith?
data.answer: We are obliged to make open profession of our faith as often as God's honor, our neighbor's spiritual good, or our own requires it. "Whosoever," says Christ, "shall confess Me before men, I will also confess him before My Father who is in heaven."
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Which are the sins against hope?
data.answer: The sins against hope are presumption and despair.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is presumption?
data.answer: Presumption is a rash expectation of salvation without making proper use of the necessary means to obtain it.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is despair?
data.answer: Despair is the loss of hope in God's mercy.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: How do we sin against the love of God?
data.answer: We sin against the love of God by all sin, but particularly by mortal sin.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Does the first Commandment forbid the honoring of the saints?
data.answer: The first Commandment does not forbid the honoring of the saints, but rather approves of it; because by honoring the saints, who are the chosen friends of God, we honor God Himself.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Does the first Commandment forbid us to pray to the saints?
data.answer: The first Commandment does not forbid us to pray to the saints.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What do we mean by praying to the saints?
data.answer: By praying to the saints we mean the asking of their help and prayers.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: How do we know that the saints hear us?
data.answer: We know that the saints hear us, because they are with God, who makes our prayers known to them.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Why do we believe that the saints will help us?
data.answer: We believe that the saints will help us because both they and we are members of the same Church, and they love us as their brethren.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: How are the saints and we members of the same Church?
data.answer: The saints and we are members of the same Church, because the Church in heaven and the Church on earth are one and the same Church, and all its members are in communion with one another.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is the communion of the members of the Church called?
data.answer: The communion of the members of the Church is called the communion of saints.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What does the communion of saints mean?
data.answer: The communion of saints means the union which exists between the members of the Church on earth with one another, and with the blessed in heaven and with the suffering souls in purgatory.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What benefits are derived from the communion of saints?
data.answer: The following benefits are derived from the communion of saints:--the faithful on earth assist one another by their prayers and good works, and they are aided by the intercession of the saints in heaven, while both the saints in heaven and the faithful on earth help the souls in purgatory.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Does the first Commandment forbid us to honor relics?
data.answer: The first Commandment does not forbid us to honor relics, because relics are the bodies of the saints, or objects directly connected with them or with our Lord.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Does the first Commandment forbid the making of images?
data.answer: The first Commandment does forbid the making of images if they are made to be adored as gods, but it does not forbid the making of them to put us in mind of Jesus Christ, His Blessed Mother, and the saints.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Is it right to show respect to the pictures and images of Christ and His saints?
data.answer: It is right to show respect to the pictures and images of Christ and His saints, because they are the representations and memorials of them.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Is it allowed to pray to the crucifix or to the images and relics of the saints?
data.answer: It is not allowed to pray to the crucifix or images and relics of the saints, for they have no life, nor power to help us, nor sense to hear us.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Why do we pray before the crucifix and the images and relics of the saints?
data.answer: We pray before the crucifix and images and relics of the saints because they enliven our devotion by exciting pious affections and desires, and by reminding us of Christ and of the saints, that we may imitate their virtues.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is the second Commandment?
data.answer: The second Commandment is: Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord thy God in vain.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What are we commanded by the second Commandment?
data.answer: We are commanded by the second Commandment to speak with reverence of God and of the saints, and of all holy things, and to keep our lawful oaths and vows.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is an oath?
data.answer: An oath is the calling upon God to witness the truth of what we say.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: When may we take an oath?
data.answer: We may take an oath when it is ordered by lawful authority or required for God's honor or for our own or our neighbor's good.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is necessary to make an oath lawful?
data.answer: To make an oath lawful it is necessary that what we swear to, be true, and that there be a sufficient cause for taking an oath.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is a vow?
data.answer: A vow is a deliberate promise made to God to do something that is pleasing to Him.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Is it a sin not to fulfill our vows?
data.answer: Not to fulfill our vows is a sin, mortal or venial, according to the nature of the vow and the intention we had in making it.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is forbidden by the second Commandment?
data.answer: The second Commandment forbids all false, rash, unjust, and unnecessary oaths, blasphemy, cursing, and profane words.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is the third Commandment?
data.answer: The third Commandment is: Remember thou keep holy the Sabbath day.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What are we commanded by the third Commandment?
data.answer: By the third Commandment we are commanded to keep holy the Lord's day and the holydays of obligation, on which we are to give our time to the service and worship of God.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: How are we to worship God on Sundays and holydays of obligation?
data.answer: We are to worship God on Sundays and holydays of obligation by hearing Mass, by prayer, and by other good works.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Are the Sabbath day and the Sunday the same?
data.answer: The Sabbath day and the Sunday are not the same. The Sabbath is the seventh day of the week, and is the day which was kept holy in the Old Law; the Sunday is the first day of the week, and is the day which is kept holy in the New Law.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Why does the Church command us to keep the Sunday holy instead of the Sabbath?
data.answer: The Church commands us to keep the Sunday holy instead of the Sabbath because on Sunday Christ rose from the dead, and on Sunday He sent the Holy Ghost upon the Apostles.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is forbidden by the third Commandment?
data.answer: The third Commandment forbids all unnecessary servile work and whatever else may hinder the due observance of the Lord's day.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What are servile works?
data.answer: Servile works are those which require labor rather of body than of mind.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Are servile works on Sunday ever lawful?
data.answer: Servile works are lawful on Sunday when the honor of God, the good of our neighbor, or necessity requires them.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is the fourth Commandment?
data.answer: The fourth Commandment is: Honor thy father and thy mother.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What are we commanded by the fourth Commandment?
data.answer: We are commanded by the fourth Commandment to honor, love, and obey our parents in all that is not sin.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Are we bound to honor and obey others than our parents?
data.answer: We are also bound to honor and obey our bishops, pastors, magistrates, teachers, and other lawful superiors.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Have parents and superiors any duties towards those who are under their charge?
data.answer: It is the duty of parents and superiors to take good care of all under their charge and give them proper direction and example.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is forbidden by the fourth Commandment?
data.answer: The fourth Commandment forbids all disobedience, contempt, and stubbornness towards our parents or lawful superiors.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is the fifth Commandment?
data.answer: The fifth Commandment is: Thou shalt not kill.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What are we commanded by the fifth Commandment?
data.answer: We are commanded by the fifth Commandment to live in peace and union with our neighbor, to respect his rights, to seek his spiritual and bodily welfare, and to take proper care of our own life and health.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is forbidden by the fifth Commandment?
data.answer: The fifth Commandment forbids all wilful murder, fighting, anger, hatred, revenge, and bad example.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is the sixth Commandment?
data.answer: The sixth Commandment is: Thou shalt not commit adultery.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What are we commanded by the sixth Commandment?
data.answer: We are commanded by the sixth Commandment to be pure in thought and modest in all our looks, words, and actions.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is forbidden by the sixth Commandment?
data.answer: The sixth commandment forbids all unchaste freedom with another's wife or husband; also all immodesty with ourselves or others in looks, dress, words, or actions.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Does the sixth Commandment forbid the reading of bad and immodest books and newspapers?
data.answer: The sixth Commandment does forbid the reading of bad and immodest books and newspapers.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is the seventh Commandment?
data.answer: The seventh Commandment is: Thou shalt not steal.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What are we commanded by the seventh Commandment?
data.answer: By the seventh Commandment we are commanded to give to all men what belongs to them and to respect their property.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is forbidden by the seventh Commandment?
data.answer: The seventh Commandment forbids all unjust taking or keeping what belongs to another.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Are we bound to restore ill-gotten goods?
data.answer: We are bound to restore ill-gotten goods, or the value of them, as far as we are able; otherwise we cannot be forgiven.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Are we obliged to repair the damage we have unjustly caused?
data.answer: We are bound to repair the damage we have unjustly caused.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is the eighth Commandment?
data.answer: The eighth Commandment is: Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbor.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What are we commanded by the eighth Commandment?
data.answer: We are commanded by the eighth Commandment to speak the truth in all things and to be careful of the honor and reputation of every one.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is forbidden by the eighth Commandment?
data.answer: The eighth Commandment forbids all rash judgments, backbiting, slanders, and lies.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What must they do who have lied about their neighbor and seriously injured his character?
data.answer: They who have lied about their neighbor and seriously injured his character must repair the injury done as far as they are able, otherwise they will not be forgiven.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is the ninth Commandment?
data.answer: The ninth Commandment is: Thou shalt not covet thy neighbor's wife.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What are we commanded by the ninth Commandment?
data.answer: We are commanded by the ninth Commandment to keep ourselves pure in thought and desire.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is forbidden by the ninth Commandment?
data.answer: The ninth Commandment forbids unchaste thoughts, desires of another's wife or husband, and all other unlawful impure thoughts and desires.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Are impure thoughts and desires always sins?
data.answer: Impure thoughts and desires are always sins, unless they displease us and we try to banish them.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is the tenth Commandment?
data.answer: The tenth Commandment is: Thou shalt not covet thy neighbor's goods.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What are we commanded by the tenth Commandment?
data.answer: By the tenth Commandment we are commanded to be content with what we have, and to rejoice in our neighbor's welfare.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is forbidden by the tenth Commandment?
data.answer: The tenth Commandment forbids all desires to take or keep wrongfully what belongs to another.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Which are the chief commandments of the Church?
data.answer: The chief commandments of the Church are six:
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Is it a mortal sin not to hear Mass on a Sunday or a holyday of obligation?
data.answer: It is a mortal sin not to hear Mass on a Sunday or a holyday of obligation, unless we are excused for a serious reason. They also commit a mortal sin who, having others under their charge, hinder them from hearing Mass, without a sufficient reason.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Why were holydays instituted by the Church?
data.answer: Holydays were instituted by the Church to recall to our minds the great mysteries of religion and the virtues and rewards of the saints.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: How should we keep the holydays of obligation?
data.answer: We should keep the holydays of obligation as we should keep the Sunday.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What do you mean by fast-days?
data.answer: By fast-days I mean days on which we are allowed but one full meal.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What do you mean by days of abstinence?
data.answer: By days of abstinence I mean days on which we are forbidden to eat flesh-meat, but are allowed the usual number of meals.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Why does the Church command us to fast and abstain?
data.answer: The Church commands us to fast and abstain, in order that we may mortify our passions and satisfy for our sins.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Why does the Church command us to abstain from flesh-meat on Fridays?
data.answer: The Church commands us to abstain from flesh-meat on Fridays, in honor of the day on which our Saviour died.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is meant by the command of confessing at least once a year?
data.answer: By the command of confessing at least once a year is meant that we are obliged, under pain of mortal sin, to go to confession within the year.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Should we confess only once a year?
data.answer: We should confess frequently, if we wish to lead a good life.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Should children go to Confession?
data.answer: Children should go to Confession when they are old enough to commit sin, which is commonly about the age of seven years.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What sin does he commit who neglects to receive Communion during the Easter time?
data.answer: He who neglects to receive Communion during the Easter time commits a mortal sin.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is the Easter time?
data.answer: The Easter time is, in this country, the time between the first Sunday of Lent and Trinity Sunday.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Are we obliged to contribute to the support of our pastors?
data.answer: We are obliged to contribute to the support of our pastors, and to bear our share in the expenses of the church and school.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is the meaning of the commandment not to marry within the third degree of kindred?
data.answer: The meaning of the commandment not to marry within the third degree of kindred is that no one is allowed to marry another within the third degree of blood relationship.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is the meaning of the command not to marry privately?
data.answer: The command not to marry privately means that none should marry without the blessing of God's priests or without witnesses.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is the meaning of the precept not to solemnize marriage at forbidden times?
data.answer: The meaning of the precept not to solemnize marriage at forbidden times is that during Lent and Advent the marriage ceremony should not be performed with pomp or a nuptial Mass.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is the nuptial Mass?
data.answer: A nuptial Mass is a Mass appointed by the Church to invoke a special blessing upon the married couple.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Should Catholics be married at a nuptial Mass?
data.answer: Catholics should be married at a nuptial Mass, because they thereby show greater reverence for the holy Sacrament and bring richer blessings upon their wedded life.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: When will Christ judge us?
data.answer: Christ will judge us immediately after our death, and on the last day.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is the judgment called which we have to undergo immediately after death?
data.answer: The judgment we have to undergo immediately after death is called the Particular Judgment.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is the judgment called which all men have to undergo on the last day?
data.answer: The judgment which all men have to undergo on the last day is called the General Judgment.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Why does Christ judge men immediately after death?
data.answer: Christ judges men immediately after death to reward or punish them according to their deeds.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What are the rewards or punishments appointed for men's souls after the Particular Judgment?
data.answer: The rewards or punishments appointed for men's souls after the Particular Judgment are Heaven, Purgatory, and Hell.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is Hell?
data.answer: Hell is a state to which the wicked are condemned, and in which they are deprived of the sight of God for all eternity, and are in dreadful torments.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is Purgatory?
data.answer: Purgatory is a state in which those suffer for a time who die guilty of venial sins, or without having satisfied for the punishment due to their sins.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Can the faithful on earth help the souls in Purgatory?
data.answer: The faithful on earth can help the souls in Purgatory by their prayers, fasts, alms-deeds; by indulgences, and by having Masses said for them.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: If every one is judged immediately after death, what need is there of a General Judgment?
data.answer: There is need of a General Judgment, though every one is judged immediately after death, that the providence of God, which, on earth, often permits the good to suffer and the wicked to prosper, may in the end appear just before all men.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Will our bodies share in the reward or punishment of our souls?
data.answer: Our bodies will share in the reward or punishment of our souls, because through the resurrection they will again be united to them.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: In what state will the bodies of the just rise?
data.answer: The bodies of the just will rise glorious and immortal.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: Will the bodies of the damned also rise?
data.answer: The bodies of the damned will also rise, but they will be condemned to eternal punishment.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What is Heaven?
data.answer: Heaven is the state of everlasting life in which we see God face to face, are made like unto Him in glory, and enjoy eternal happiness.
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD

data.question: What words should we bear always in mind?
data.answer: We should bear always in mind these words of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ: "What doth it profit a man if he gain the whole world and suffer the loss of his own soul, or what exchange shall a man give for his soul? For the Son of man shall come in the glory of His Father with His angels; and then will He render to every man according to his works."
data.group: LESSON TWENTY-NINTH: ON THE COMMANDMENTS OF GOD
